title: sphacele salviae
intro: Sphacele salviae, also known as sage, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as thujone, camphor, and borneol, which exhibit medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of sphacele salviae include improving cognitive function, memory, and mood, as well as providing relief from menopausal symptoms and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of sphacele salviae can be found in teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
